pattern: Ensure captions are readable on *all* backgrounds by using a solid background block or a high-contrast text color with a subtle scrim, especially over busy or bright footage.
Captions must be positioned to avoid collision or close proximity with on-screen app UI text elements.
Strictly adhere to brand color guidelines for *all* visual elements: backgrounds, props, character attire, and UI highlights. Avoid dominant off-brand colors.
Never feature competitor logos, UI, products, or any identifiable competitor branding in any ad creative.
Captions placed in the bottom 25% of the frame collide with app UI buttons; use the top half or add a translucent scrim to create a clear safe zone.
All brand assets (logos, taglines, app screens) must be fully visible, uncropped, and use the official, approved versions. Never crop the app screen or obscure the logo.
Static text cards or visuals significantly reduce engagement and should be replaced with dynamic content to maintain viewer interest.
Remove all recording artifacts (e.g., screen recording UI, watermarks, timestamps) to maintain a professional appearance.
Keep captions to a maximum of 1-2 lines for quick readability in fast-scrolling feeds; avoid dense paragraphs.
The final call-to-action (CTA) or outro must exclusively use the official, pre-rendered brand logo-reveal video.
Screen recordings used in ads must exhibit clear lighting and high visual clarity.
Captions must not obscure critical visual information or overlays.
Captions require sufficient contrast against their background; consider using a scrim or solid background for complex or light scenes.
Chaotic or unprofessional transition effects negatively impact readability and brand perception.
Opening shots should be dynamic or visually interesting to maximize hook strength.
Avoid low-quality, amateur, or poorly framed footage; ensure all visuals are professional and well-produced.
Visually demonstrate app functionality rather than relying on abstract text descriptions.
Integrate brand colors early in the ad to establish brand presence.
Ensure a consistent visual style across all frames to avoid a disjointed or unprofessional appearance.
